title: Fix infinite loading screen — app stuck on 'Loading your profile...'

requirements.summary: Fix the infinite 'Loading your profile...' state by adding timeout and error fallback logic to the profile-fetching hook and App.tsx loading guard
acceptanceCriteria: Opening the app no longer results in an indefinite 'Loading your profile...' spinner.
If the profile query fails or times out, the app falls back to the login/unauthenticated state within 5 seconds.
After the fallback, the user can see and interact with the login button to authenticate via Internet Identity.
No runtime errors are thrown during the loading-to-login fallback transition.

file_operations.description: Add a 5-second timeout mechanism to useGetCallerUserProfile hook using a custom timer that automatically marks the query as failed if it doesn't resolve within the timeout window. Update the hook to return an explicit error state when the timeout is reached, allowing downstream components to detect the failure and fall back to the login screen. Ensure the timeout is cleared when the query completes successfully or when the component unmounts. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.

Refactor the loading guard logic to check for query timeout/error states in addition to the existing loading checks. If the profile query has errored or timed out (as indicated by the updated useGetCallerUserProfile hook), immediately render the unauthenticated login screen instead of continuing to show the loading spinner. Ensure the guard does not block rendering when the actor or identity initialization is incomplete — use the actorFetching flag and query.isFetched to distinguish between initial actor setup and profile-fetch completion. Add error boundary handling around the profile fetch to catch any runtime exceptions and fall back gracefully to the login state.
